description: Stable contract identifier for Q-DESN plus exDQLM/DQLM dynamic fit and forecast validation.
Article-facing schema version. Active value is rolling_origin_v3_lead_interface_v1.
Validation study identifier.
Versioned run tag for the producing validation run.
Stable atomic run specification identifier for targeted reruns and provenance.
Pre-override atomic run specification identifier when row overrides are used.
Named calibration profile for the state-space model and inference settings.
Compact hash of clock prior covariance and discount settings.
Top-level model family such as qdesn or exdqlm_dqlm.
Model variant such as qdesn_ridge qdesn_rhs_ns dqlm or exdqlm.
Inference method alias such as vb or mcmc.
Inference method such as vb or mcmc.
Staged launch phase.
Atomic execution stage such as all fit-only forecast-only or metrics-only.
Explicit row status.
Stage or terminal failure class for failed rows.
Explicit failure reason or empty for successful rows.
Number of warnings attached to the row when available.
Compact semicolon-delimited diagnostic flags.
Health or signoff gate status.
Human/study-facing signoff grade where available.
Frozen shared source registry identifier.
Frozen shared source registry root.
Frozen shared source registry path alias.
Name of the registry hash being enforced.
Expected registry hash value.
Expected registry hash alias.
Source cell identifier.
Simulation scenario identifier.
Path to the source series file when available.
Hash for the source series file when available.
Path to the true quantile grid when available.
Hash for the true quantile grid when available.
Observation family.
Observation family alias used by article guards.
Quantile level.
Filesystem-safe quantile label.
Nominal fit window size.
Effective fit window size used by the model.
Warmup length in the frozen source design.
Main source length in the frozen source design.
Total generated source length.
Human-readable fit size label such as TT500 or TT5000.
Latent state clock convention used to align the model to the source window.
Latent clock index used to initialize the first fitted source row.
Offset between source training start and latent model clock start.
Period used by the dynamic harmonic state-space model.
Harmonic orders used by the dynamic state-space model.
Base prior covariance scale used by the dynamic state-space model.
Prior covariance scale used for trend states.
Prior covariance scale used for harmonic states.
Discount factor value or comma-delimited discount factors.
State block dimensions associated with discount factors.
Initial forecast origin source index.
Training source start index.
Training source end index.
Forecast protocol identifier.
State update method used for rolling-origin forecasts.
Whether the model was refit at each forecast origin.
Whether observed y values through the current origin are used to update state or lags.
Whether true quantiles were used in model fitting. Must be false for primary runs.
Maximum rolling-origin forecast lead configured for the run.
Rolling-origin source-index stride used for the run.
Initial forecast origin source index compatibility alias.
Forecast block start source index compatibility alias.
Forecast block end source index compatibility alias.
Forecast block start source index.
Forecast block end source index.
Forecast block length.
First rolling forecast origin contributing to this lead row.
Last rolling forecast origin contributing to this lead row.
Lead r for the lead-level rolling-origin row.
First scored target source index for this lead.
Last scored target source index for this lead.
Number of rolling origins scored for this lead.
H=100 compatibility summary start source index.
H=100 compatibility summary end source index.
H=100 compatibility metric sample size.
H=100 compatibility quantile MAE.
H=100 compatibility quantile RMSE.
H=100 compatibility mean pinball loss.
H=1000 compatibility summary start source index.
H=1000 compatibility summary end source index.

Fit metric sample size.

Fit coverage minus tau.

Lead-level forecast true-quantile RMSE.
Lead-level forecast true-quantile bias.
